# SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION

A professional family law mediation practice focused on helping families navigate legal disputes — such as divorce, child custody, and asset division — through structured, neutral mediation rather than adversarial litigation. The practice emphasizes resolution, empathy, and a calm, trustworthy environment to guide clients through emotionally challenging legal processes.

Source: https://saldivar-family-law-mediation-ecfb3105.zappy5.app

# Resolve Family Disputes with Calm and Clarity


### Guiding families through divorce, custody, and asset division with neutral, dignified mediation — not courtroom battles.



Schedule a Consultation
Explore Our Services














## A Clear Path Through Complex Family Transitions


Family law mediation offers a private, structured alternative to adversarial litigation. Our practice provides a neutral, empathetic environment where families can resolve divorce, custody, and asset matters with dignity and clarity — guided by an experienced mediator focused on sustainable outcomes, not courtroom conflict.










### Divorce Mediation


Navigate the legal and emotional complexities of divorce with a neutral guide who helps both parties reach a fair, mutually acceptable agreement — preserving respect and minimizing the toll on your family.










### Child Custody & Parenting Plans


Create thoughtful, child-centered custody arrangements and parenting plans that prioritize stability and emotional well-being, crafted through collaborative dialogue rather than contested hearings.










### Property & Asset Division


Achieve an equitable division of marital property, real estate, retirement accounts, and complex assets with transparency and financial clarity — avoiding the uncertainty of a judge-imposed settlement.










### Support & Family Agreements


Develop clear, enforceable agreements for spousal support, child support, and broader family arrangements that reflect your family's unique needs — built on mutual understanding, not litigation pressure.

















## Our Mediation Process


A structured, five-step approach to guide your family toward a fair and lasting resolution. Every stage is designed to reduce conflict and provide clarity.













1



### Initial Conversation


We begin with a confidential, no-obligation discussion to understand your situation, explain the mediation process, and determine if it's the right path for your family.







2



### Information Gathering


We collect all relevant financial documents, parenting plans, and legal details to create a complete, transparent picture for both parties.







3



### Joint Mediation Sessions


In structured, neutral sessions, we facilitate open dialogue to identify shared interests and explore creative solutions for custody, support, and asset division.







4



### Drafting Agreements


We translate your decisions into a clear, legally sound Memorandum of Understanding, ready for review by independent legal counsel.







5



### Final Resolution Path


We finalize the agreement and provide a clear roadmap for the future, ensuring both parties can move forward with confidence and closure.




















## Guided by Principle. Focused on Resolution.


As a dedicated family law mediation practice, we provide a structured, neutral environment where families can resolve disputes with dignity. Our approach replaces courtroom conflict with collaborative problem-solving, helping you move forward with clarity and confidence.









### Professional Neutrality


As a neutral third party, the mediator does not take sides or impose decisions. We facilitate open dialogue, ensuring every voice is heard and respected throughout the process.











### Family-Focused Approach


We prioritize the well-being of the entire family unit, especially children. Our mediation sessions are designed to reduce emotional strain and foster respectful communication.











### Confidential Structured Guidance


With extensive experience in complex family dynamics, we provide a confidential framework that keeps discussions productive, organized, and forward-looking.





























## Begin with a confidential conversation


Every family's situation is unique. In a private, no-obligation call we'll listen to where you are, answer your questions about family law mediation, and help you decide whether this path is right for you.


Schedule a Consultation
Explore Mediation Services













Guiding families toward resolution with clarity, dignity, and lasting peace of mind.

# Family Law Mediation


A private, structured path to resolution — without the courtroom.









## Guiding Families Toward Practical Agreements


When families face legal transitions, the adversarial court system often deepens conflict and prolongs uncertainty. At Saldivar Family Law Mediation, we offer a different approach — one grounded in neutrality, respect, and lasting solutions. Our mediation practice helps couples and families navigate divorce, child custody, and asset division with clarity and compassion, preserving relationships and reducing the emotional and financial toll of litigation.


Mediation is not about winning or losing. It is about finding common ground. In a confidential, structured setting, we facilitate productive conversations that allow both parties to shape their own agreements — agreements that reflect their unique priorities and the best interests of their children. The result is a more peaceful transition and a foundation for healthier family dynamics moving forward.








We handle all aspects of family law mediation — from initial separation to final agreement — always prioritizing dignity, privacy, and the well-being of everyone involved.


















Our Practice Areas

## Comprehensive Family Law Mediation


Guiding families through structured, neutral mediation across six core areas of family law — designed to preserve relationships, reduce conflict, and create lasting resolutions.








### Divorce Mediation


Navigate the dissolution of marriage with dignity and mutual respect. Our structured process helps couples reach fair agreements on all terms of divorce without courtroom battles.

Marital dissolution, legal separation, uncontested divorce






### Parenting Plans


Develop thoughtful, child-centered parenting arrangements that prioritize stability and well-being. We help parents create detailed plans covering decision-making, holidays, and daily routines.

Co-parenting frameworks, decision-making authority, holiday schedules






### Custody Schedules


Create practical physical and legal custody schedules that work for your family's unique circumstances. We facilitate balanced discussions around parenting time, school calendars, and extracurricular activities.

Physical custody, legal custody, visitation, school-year planning






### Support Discussions


Address spousal support and child support obligations through transparent, guided conversations. We use neutral financial frameworks to help both parties understand reasonable support parameters.

Spousal support, child support, guideline calculations, modification






### Asset & Debt Division


Achieve equitable distribution of marital property, retirement accounts, real estate, and liabilities. Our mediation approach brings clarity to complex financial landscapes without adversarial positioning.

Property division, retirement accounts, real estate, business interests






### Unmarried Couple Agreements


Protect your interests in non-marital relationships through clear cohabitation agreements and post-separation mediation. We help unmarried partners define rights, responsibilities, and fair outcomes.

Cohabitation agreements, property rights, post-separation mediation




Every family situation is unique. Schedule a confidential consultation to discuss how mediation can work for your specific circumstances.

Discuss Your Case









## Why Families Choose Mediation


Family law mediation offers a path forward that preserves relationships, reduces emotional strain, and puts decision-making power back in your hands. Discover how our structured, neutral process can make a meaningful difference for your family.








### Complete Privacy & Confidentiality


Unlike public court proceedings, family law mediation takes place in a private, confidential setting. Your personal matters, financial details, and family dynamics remain protected, allowing you to speak openly without fear of public record. This discretion is especially valuable when addressing sensitive issues like asset division or parenting arrangements.







Your story stays between us








### Reduced Conflict, Preserved Relationships


Litigation is inherently adversarial — it pits parties against each other and often deepens existing wounds. Mediation shifts the focus from winning to resolving. By fostering respectful communication and mutual understanding, we help families — especially those who will co-parent for years to come — build a foundation for healthier future interactions.







Resolution without hostility








### Greater Control Over Your Family's Future


In court, a judge who doesn't know your family makes the final decisions. In mediation, you and the other party craft the agreement. This collaborative approach ensures the outcome reflects your unique values, priorities, and children's needs — not a one-size-fits-all legal ruling. You maintain autonomy over your family's path forward.







You decide, together








### Structured Support for Co-Parenting


For separating parents, the relationship doesn't end — it transforms. Our mediation process helps establish clear, practical parenting plans that prioritize your children's emotional well-being. We guide you through creating schedules, communication protocols, and decision-making frameworks that reduce friction and support your children's stability during transition.







Putting children first








### Efficient Resolution of Financial Matters


Litigation can drain family resources through prolonged legal fees and court costs. Mediation is typically faster and significantly more cost-effective. We help you navigate complex financial landscapes — property division, support obligations, business valuations — with transparency and practicality, preserving more of your assets for your family's future.







Protecting your family's resources











Common Questions

## Frequently Asked Questions About Family Law Mediation


Clear answers to help you understand the mediation process and what to expect as you move toward resolution.








01

### Who should attend mediation sessions?



Mediation typically involves the parties directly involved in the dispute — for example, both spouses in a divorce or both parents in a custody matter. In some cases, other family members or support persons may attend with the agreement of all parties. Your mediator will discuss who should be present during your initial consultation to ensure the sessions remain productive and balanced.






02

### Do I need an attorney if I choose mediation?



While mediation is designed to help you reach agreements without courtroom litigation, you are always encouraged to consult with an independent attorney. Many clients choose to have a consulting attorney review proposed agreements before signing. The mediator remains neutral and does not provide legal advice to either party, which is why having your own legal counsel can provide valuable peace of mind.






03

### Is everything discussed in mediation confidential?



Yes. Mediation is a confidential process under Texas law. Discussions, offers, and statements made during mediation generally cannot be used as evidence in court if the case does not settle. This confidentiality encourages open, honest communication and allows both parties to explore solutions without fear that their words will be used against them later.






04

### How long does the mediation process typically take?



The timeline varies depending on the complexity of the issues and the willingness of both parties to collaborate. Some families reach a full agreement in a single session, while others benefit from two to four sessions spaced over several weeks. At Saldivar Family Law Mediation, we work at a pace that respects everyone's emotional readiness while keeping the process moving forward efficiently.






05

### Can mediation work in high-conflict situations?



Absolutely. In fact, mediation can be especially valuable when emotions are running high. A skilled mediator is trained to manage difficult dynamics, ensure each person is heard, and keep conversations focused on practical solutions rather than past grievances. With the right structure and ground rules, even deeply contentious disputes can find a path to resolution through mediation.






06

### What happens after we reach an agreement?



Once both parties agree on all terms, the mediator will prepare a detailed written summary of the agreements reached. This document can then be reviewed by your respective attorneys and, if appropriate, submitted to the court for approval and entry as a binding order. The goal is to leave mediation with a clear, actionable plan that brings lasting closure to your family law matter.







Still have questions? We're here to help you understand your options.

Schedule a Consultation











## Begin with a confidential conversation


Every family situation is unique. Let's discuss your circumstances in a private, no-obligation consultation to determine whether family law mediation is the right path forward for you and your loved ones.

Schedule Your Consultation












Guiding families toward resolution with clarity, dignity, and lasting peace of mind.

## Meet Your Trusted Mediation Advocate — SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION

URL: https://saldivar-family-law-mediation-ecfb3105.zappy5.app/about
Description: Discover the empathetic expertise behind Saldivar Family Law Mediation. We guide families through legal crossroads with integrity, calm, and a commitment to fair, lasting solutions. Learn our story.

Meet Your Trusted Mediation Advocate — SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION






























Skip to main content

















- Home
- Mediation Services
- About the Practice



Contact



















# Maria Saldivar


Founder & Lead Mediator





With over fifteen years of experience in family law mediation, Maria Saldivar has dedicated her career to helping families navigate some of life's most challenging transitions. A former family law attorney, she witnessed firsthand how adversarial litigation can deepen conflict and prolong emotional distress — especially when children are involved. That realization led her to establish Saldivar Family Law Mediation, a practice built on the belief that families deserve a better path forward.


Maria's approach is grounded in neutrality, empathy, and a deep respect for each family's unique dynamics. She creates a structured yet compassionate environment where all parties feel heard, guiding them toward mutually acceptable solutions for divorce, child custody, and asset division. Her goal is not simply to reach an agreement, but to help families build a foundation for healthier communication long after the mediation concludes.



## A Commitment to Respectful Resolution


Every mediation session is guided by three core principles: preserving dignity, prioritizing the well-being of children, and empowering families to shape their own futures. Maria believes that even in the midst of conflict, there is an opportunity for clarity, healing, and a respectful transition to the next chapter.


Schedule a Consultation












## Our Philosophy


We believe that even the most difficult family transitions deserve a process rooted in respect, clarity, and dignity. Our approach to family law mediation is built on a foundation of core values that guide every conversation we facilitate.











### Neutrality


We maintain a balanced, impartial presence in every session. As a family law mediation attorney, our role is not to take sides but to create a structured space where both parties can be heard equally, fostering an environment where fair outcomes can emerge naturally.









### Dignity


Every individual deserves to navigate family transitions with their self-respect intact. We uphold a standard of courtesy and compassion that protects the emotional well-being of everyone involved, ensuring that the mediation process never adds unnecessary harm to an already challenging situation.









### Clarity & Child-Centered Focus


We cut through legal complexity with plain language and transparent guidance. Above all, we keep the best interests of children at the heart of every decision, helping parents craft parenting plans and agreements that prioritize stability, security, and long-term well-being for their families.















Credentials & Experience

## Qualified to Guide Your Family Through Resolution


With decades of combined experience in family law and conflict resolution, our practice is built on a foundation of rigorous training, ethical standards, and a deep commitment to helping families find peaceful, lasting solutions.










### Advanced Mediation Certification


Certified by the Texas Mediator Credentialing Association (TMCA) with advanced specialization in family and divorce mediation, ensuring adherence to the highest professional standards.











### Juris Doctor, Family Law Focus


Graduate of Southern Methodist University Dedman School of Law with a concentrated curriculum in family law, child advocacy, and alternative dispute resolution.











### Member, Texas Association of Mediators


Active member of the Texas Association of Mediators (TAM) and the Family Law Section of the State Bar of Texas, staying current with evolving family law statutes and best practices.











### Conflict Resolution Training


Completed over 300 hours of specialized training in interest-based negotiation, domestic violence screening, and child-inclusive mediation processes to ensure every family member's voice is heard.




















## What Our Clients Say








I felt genuinely heard for the first time during this entire process. The mediation gave us a clear path forward without the bitterness of court.


Maria G.
Divorce Mediation Client








We were able to reach a parenting plan that actually works for our kids. The structured sessions kept us focused on solutions, not blame.


David & Rachel T.
Child Custody Mediation








The practical approach to dividing assets saved us thousands in legal fees. More importantly, it preserved a working relationship for co-parenting.


Carlos M.
Asset Division Client

# Let's Find a Path Forward Together


Reaching out is the first step toward resolution. Our practice is built on empathy, clarity, and a deep commitment to guiding families through difficult transitions with dignity. We welcome your questions and are ready to listen.









## What to Expect








### A Safe, Neutral Space


Every conversation is confidential and grounded in respect. Our office provides a calm environment where you can speak openly without judgment.










### Your Initial Consultation


We'll discuss your situation, explain how family law mediation works, and answer your questions. There is no pressure — only an honest assessment of whether mediation is right for your family.










### Structured, Fair Process


Mediation keeps decision-making in your hands. We guide the conversation, ensure both voices are heard, and work toward durable agreements — without the cost and conflict of court.









## Contact Our Office


We serve families throughout the region. Inquiries are typically answered within one business day.

- [business_email]

- [business_address]







All communications are strictly confidential.

Our Practice


## A calm, private space for difficult conversations





Our office is intentionally designed to feel less like a law firm and more like a comfortable, neutral living room. Soft lighting, natural textures, and private meeting rooms create an environment where you can speak openly without feeling intimidated.









### Complete Privacy


Soundproofed rooms and strict confidentiality protocols ensure your discussions remain protected.










### Remote Availability


We offer secure video mediation sessions for clients who prefer to meet from the comfort of their home.







### What to expect when you reach out


Your first contact is always handled with respect and discretion. You'll speak directly with our intake coordinator, who will listen without judgment, explain the mediation process clearly, and help you determine if family law mediation is right for your situation. We typically respond within one business day.



Schedule a Consultation



























### Our Office





Providing compassionate family law mediation services throughout the greater metropolitan area. We offer both in-person and virtual mediation sessions to accommodate your family's needs.















Guiding families toward resolution with clarity, dignity, and lasting peace of mind.

# Privacy Policy



Last Updated:  6/14/2026



SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION ("Company", "we", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This privacy policy explains how we collect, use and protect your information when you use our website [business_website] ("Service").


## 1. Information Collection


We may collect the following types of information:


- Personal Information: Name, email address, phone number and address

- Technical Information: IP address, browser type, operating system and website usage data

- Cookies: Information stored on your computer to improve user experience




## 2. Use of Information


We use the collected information for the following purposes:


- Providing requested services

- Improving service and user experience

- Contacting you regarding our services

- Compliance with legal requirements




## 3. Information Sharing


We do not sell, rent or share your personal information with third parties, except in the following cases:


- With your explicit consent

- When required by law

- To protect our rights or user safety

- With service providers who assist in operating the service (under confidentiality agreements)




## 4. User Rights


In accordance with Israeli Privacy Protection Law 1981, you have the following rights:


- Right to access personal information stored about you

- Right to correct inaccurate or incomplete information

- Right to delete personal information in certain circumstances

- Right to object to information processing for marketing purposes

- Right to file a complaint with the Data Protection Authority




## 5. Cookies


Our website uses cookies to improve user experience. You can manage cookies through your browser settings. Disabling cookies may affect website functionality.


## 6. Information Security


We implement reasonable security measures to protect your information, including encryption, firewalls and restricted access control. However, no transmission or storage method is 100% secure.


## 7. Policy Changes


We may update this privacy policy from time to time. Material changes will be posted on the website with 30 days advance notice.



## 8. Contact Information


For questions or requests regarding this privacy policy, please contact us:


- Company: SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION

- Email: [business_email]

- Phone: [business_phone]

- Address: [business_address]



Data Protection Authority: For complaints, you may contact the Data Protection Authority at the Ministry of Justice

# Accessibility Statement



Last updated:  June 14, 2026



SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION is committed to making our services accessible to all users. We strive to implement the Israeli Standard (TI 5568) based on WCAG 2.1 Level AA guidelines.


Accessibility Level: AA (Israeli Standard TI 5568)



## 1. Accessibility Policy


We believe in equal rights and the right of every person to receive information and services independently and equally. This website was designed and built with special emphasis on accessibility for people with disabilities.


## 2. Accessibility Arrangements on the Site


- Keyboard navigation support

- Screen reader compatibility

- Appropriate color contrast

- Flexible and scalable font sizes

- Descriptive image alt text

- Hierarchical heading structure

- Clear and descriptive links

- Support for 200% zoom without content loss





### WCAG 2.1 Compliance


The website complies with WCAG 2.1 Level AA, which includes:


- Perceivable: Information is presented in ways users can perceive

- Operable: Interface components are operable

- Understandable: Information and user interface are understandable

- Robust: Content can be interpreted by a variety of technologies





## 3. Supported Assistive Technologies


- NVDA (Windows)

- JAWS (Windows)

- VoiceOver (Mac/iOS)

- TalkBack (Android)

- Dragon NaturallySpeaking

- Switch navigation




## 4. Accessibility Testing


The website has been tested using:


- Automated accessibility testing tools

- Manual testing with screen readers

- Keyboard-only navigation

- Color contrast verification

- Testing with users with disabilities




## 5. Continuous Improvements


We are continuously working to improve the accessibility of our website and update it according to technological developments and user feedback.



## 6. Contact and Feedback


If you encounter an accessibility issue on the site or need assistance, please contact us:


- Company: SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION

- Email: [business_email]

- Phone: [business_phone]



Response time: We respond to accessibility inquiries within 3 business days and work to resolve issues within 10 business days.

# Terms of Use



Last updated:  June 14, 2026



Use of this site constitutes agreement to the terms outlined below. Please read carefully before use.



## 1. Definitions


- "Company" - SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION

- "User" - Anyone who browses or uses the site

- "Services" - Legal Services / Mediation provided through the site




## 2. Description of Services


SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION provides Legal Services / Mediation through the site. The services are subject to the terms detailed in this document and may change from time to time.


## 3. Terms of Use


Use of the site is conditional on compliance with the following terms:


- Use of the site for lawful purposes only

- No interference with site operations or other users

- No uploading of offensive, illegal, or harmful content

- Respect for copyright and intellectual property rights

- No actions that may compromise site security




## 4. User Obligations


- Providing accurate and truthful information during registration or ordering

- Maintaining confidentiality of account access details

- Immediate notification of any unauthorized account use

- Updating personal information as needed




## 5. Payment and Refund Policy


If services include payment:


- Payments are processed through secure and encrypted systems

- Prices include applicable taxes as required by law

- Right to cancel will be in accordance with consumer protection laws

- Refunds will be processed within 14 business days of cancellation approval




## 6. Limitation of Liability


Services are provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. The Company will not be liable for indirect, consequential, or special damages arising from use of the site, except where required by law.


## 7. Copyright and Intellectual Property


All content on the site, including text, images, design, and code, is protected by copyright owned by SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION. Reproduction, distribution, or commercial use of content is prohibited without written permission.


## 8. Privacy


Collection and use of personal information is subject to our Privacy Policy. Use of the site constitutes agreement to the Privacy Policy as detailed separately.


## 9. Changes to Terms of Use


The Company may change these terms of use at any time. Material changes will be posted on the site with 30 days advance notice. Continued use after changes constitutes agreement to the new terms.


## 10. Service Termination


The Company may suspend or limit service at any time, including in cases of terms of use violations. In case of service termination, relevant provisions will remain in effect.


## 11. Governing Law and Dispute Resolution


These terms of use are governed by applicable laws. Any disputes will be resolved in accordance with local jurisdiction, with preference given to arbitration or mediation where possible.


## 12. General Provisions


- If any portion of the terms is found invalid, remaining terms will remain in effect

- Non-enforcement of a right will not be considered a waiver

- These terms of use constitute the entire agreement between the parties





## 13. Contact Information


For questions or clarifications regarding the terms of use:


- Company: SALDIVAR FAMILY LAW MEDIATION

- Email: [business_email]

- Phone: [business_phone]

- Address: [business_address]
